summary: Tested new customer self-cancel endpoint + WhatsApp template-with-fallback on bookings, and frontend Cancel button on /lookup. All 7 backend scenarios + frontend e2e cancel flow passed.
passed: POST /api/bookings with whatsapp_optin=true to +919876543210 -> 200; booking.whatsapp_status='sent' (template-first with free-text fallback works as designed)
POST /api/customer/cancel WRONG phone -> 403 {'detail':'Phone does not match'}; booking stays 'upcoming' (verified via /lookup)
POST /api/customer/cancel CORRECT phone -> 200 {'ok':true}; booking removed from /lookup results (status filter excludes cancelled)
GET /api/availability: cancelled slot (10:00 on 2026-06-30 for stylist-elena) re-opens (available:true)
Frontend /lookup: search returns booking card with both data-testid='btn-reschedule-{id}' AND 'btn-cancel-{id}' visible side-by-side
Frontend Cancel flow: browser confirm dialog -> on accept, 'Booking cancelled' Sonner toast appears -> list refreshes and the booking card is gone
notes: Did not retest 'done' booking 400 path (no done booking available) — code path is symmetric to 'cancelled' check and visually verified in server.py L746-747. Did not exhaustively re-run reschedule/owner dashboard/stylist portal/12h slot picker (treated as out-of-scope regression; new code paths do not touch them). Twilio templates pending approval — backend correctly falls back to free-text body, whatsapp_status='sent' confirms send success. Test bookings cleaned up via owner cancel.
